steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the scale factor between the two radii
steps.content:
The radius of circle C is $3x$ and the radius of circle D is $87x$. To compare the areas, start with the ratio of the radii:
$$k = \frac{\text{radius D}}{\text{radius C}} = \frac{87x}{3x} = 29$$

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule
steps.content:
When a figure is scaled by a linear factor of $k$, its area is scaled by $k^2$. Here, the radius of circle D is 29 times the radius of circle C, so its area is:
$$29^2 = 841$$
times the area of circle C.
